( as by his print paper do appear ) from his study in Ʋttoxeter , otherwise and vulgar Ʋxter , in the say year do challenge all those who can write and read , to answer his 16. query concern the observation of the feast of Christmas , or the nativity of our lord and saviour Jesus Christ ; to which i John ling have be a grammar scholar , and have see somewhat in the world both in this church , and the reform beyond sea , do make this answer . the first quaere . whether such religious custom as be bind to all the church of Jesus Christ , ought not to have sure foot upon the word of God , or apostolical practice ? Resp. because i will not make paper dear by long answer or recital , i say such religious custom as be bind to all the church of Jesus Christ , aught to have footing upon the word of God , or apostolical practice . the second quaere . whether you can substantial prove that Christ be bear on the 25 of December ? and what your proof be ? Resp. it be not material , i , or no , whether i can prove that Christ be bear on the 25 of December , as it now stand in the english calendar . the three quaere . whether the celebration of that day ( grant he be bear on it ) can be clear warrant by you from scripture ? and what your scripture be ? Resp. it import no more than what be answer in the first and second . the four quaere . whether you can clear it by sound consequence from the new testament , though not set down there in totidem verbis ? Resp. it be clear by sound consequence , both from the old and new testament , that the day of the birth of our saviour , God and man , shall be keep with festivity : i will now first give you instance in the angel song to the shepherd , glory be to God on high , on earth peace , good will towards man , see the 118 psalm , 22.23 . and 24. verse , the stone which the builder refuse be the head of the corner , which be interpret in the new testament to be mean of Christ . 24. this be the day which the lord have make , we will rejoice and be glad in it , which all good interpreter say to be mean by the day of the birth of our lord and saviour Jesus Christ . many place there be in Isaiah , and other of the prophet : so that now you have better proof from scripture for keep holy a anniversary day for the celebration of the birth of our lord , then for change the sabbath to the lord day ; the proof whereof be chief ground upon apostolical custom , Isaiah , 28.16 . Math. 21.42 . act , 4.11 . Rom. 9.33 . 1. Peter , 2.6 . and 7. the five quaere . whether you can do it by universal tradition ? Resp. it|be do by universal tradition , and in that i will bring it to the customary practice of the reform church of Dutch ▪ French , and Geneva . the sixth quaere . whether ( in case it can be evidence by none of these , viz. plain text , solid inference , universal tradition ) it be not a mere humane invention , and so will worship ? and how you will one day acquit your self before God , for place , and cry up man invention , instead of the institution of Jesus Christ ? and whether it be not faithful deal with poor simple people to tell they that you have neither of these to warrant it ? Resp. i answer , it be false deal to tell Christian people that which be untrue . the seven quaere . ( since day and time command by God himself to be observe under the law , be , and be unlawful under the gospel ) whether day and time command by man , and not by God , under the gospel , be not less lawful ? CHAP. Iii . a answer to the dissuader 3 Chap. touch the original and progress of the independent in New-England . sect . I. of the title put upon we of a sect , independent . the way of the church in New-England be neither just call a sect , nor fit call independency . not a sect ; for we profess the orthodox doctrine of faith , the same with all protestant church ; we celebrate the same sacrament ; and submit to the spiritual government of the same lawful guide , so far as Christ and our own choice have set they over we . and though we do not subject our self to the government of the elder of other church , ( as many great church do , ) yet we acknowledge and reverence such church in the lord , as true church of Christ , and be willing to make use of their brotherly counsel and help as need shall require . and though we do not open the door of our church so wide , as to receive all the inhabitant of a nation , or of every town , into the fellowship of our church ; yet we do not separate from such protestant church as do take that liberty : but only we separate from the world , that be , from the worldly sort of they , who either live in open scandal , or at least doe not open hold forth any spiritual discern of the lord body , and be therefore u••eet to communicate at the lord table . nor be independency a fit name of the way of our church . for in some respect it be too strait , and in other too large ; it be too strait , in that it confine we within our self , and hold we forth as independent from all other : whereas indeed we do profess dependence upon magistrate for civil government and protection : dependence upon Christ and his word , for the sovereign government and rule of our administration : dependence upon the counsel of other church and synod ; when our own variance or ignorance may stand in need of such help from they . and therefore this title of independency straiten we , and restrain we from our necessary duty , and due liberty . again , in other respect , independency stretch it self too large , and more general , then that it can single out we . for it be compatible to a national church , as well as to a congregational . the national church of Scotland be independent from the government of the national church of England ; and so be England independent from Scotland . nor be there any sect at this day extant , but shrowd themselves under the title of independency . the Antipaedobaptists , antinomians , Familist , yea , and the seeker too , do all of they style themselves independent . nay , even the pope himself , ( who exalt himself above all civil and church-power ) yet even he also arrogate the title of independency ; prima s•des à nemine judicatur ; that be , the sea of roam be independent . why then shall independency be appropriate to we , as a character of our way , which neither true describe we , nor faithful distinguish we from many other ? wherefore if there must needs be some note of difference to decipher our estate , and to dis•inguish our way from a national churchway , i know none fit , than to denominate they classical , and we congregational . sect . ii of the number of the congregational regiment , and of the wisdom threap upon they , in engage person of note to they . the dissuader acknowledge , we be not numerous , but the few in number of the note sect , and not to consist of above one thousand person within the line of the city communication . Answ. 1. if we be the few of note Sec•• , it be sometime the lot of God Israel to be the few of all people , Deut. 7.7 . Answ. 2. if there be a thousand of our way within the line of the city communication , i hope there want not diverse more to be add to they in other part of England , beside some thousand more in New-England . but it be not for we to follow David sin in number the people of the lord ; only the lord increase their number a hundred fold , ( yea , a thousand fold ) and make they as the star of heaven for multitude . but for the quality of the person , the dissuader tell we , they have be so wise , as to engage to their party some of chief note in both house of parliament , in the assembly of divine , in the army , in the city , and countrey-committee . but in so say , the dissuader put a dishonour both upon God , and upon those person of chief note . it be a dishonour to God , to attribute that to the wisdom of man , which be the mighty and gracious work of the wisdom of God . and it be a dishonour to such man , to hold they forth as engage to this way by the wisdom and industry of man , who have be well know ( and some of they for many year ) not to have engage themselves or other any further , than the grace of Christ , and the conscience of his word have engage they to do and suffer , accord to the will of God . sect . Iii . touch the line of the pedigree of the independent in New-England . the separatist ( say the dissuader ) be their father . this be demonstrable not only by the consanguinity of their tenant , ( the one have borrow all their chief doctrine and practise from the other : ) but also by deduction of their pedigree in this clear line . mr. Robinson do derive his way to his separate congregation at Leyden ; a part of they do carry it over to Plymmouth in New-England : here mr. Cotton take it up , and do transmit it to mr. Goodwin , who do help to propagate it to sundry other in Old-England first , and after to more in holland ; till now by many hand it be sow thick in diverse part of the kingdom . Answ. that the separatist be our father , we have just deny it above ; see they neither beget we to God , nor to the church , nor to their schism . that we be ( through grace ) beget to God , and to his church , we receive ( many of we ) from the blessing of Christ upon the ministry of England . that we grow weary of the burden of episcopacy and conformity , we receive from the word of God by the help of the nonconformists there . that we lay aside the book of commonprayer , we receive from the serious meditation of the second commandment , and not from the write of the separatist , though they also have take up the same conclusion upon other premise . the particular visible church of a congregation to be the first subject of the power of the key , we receive by the light of the word from mr.
